meta.title: Iconology 1: Reflections on Eastern Orthodox Iconological Doctrine
meta.author: Joshua Schooping

questions.question: From which perspective does Schooping primarily frame this lecture?
questions.answer: Protestant evangelical

questions.question: What distinction does he insist on throughout between two kinds of visual material?
options: Old Testament vs. New Testament imagery
Religiously themed visual art vs. religious use/veneration of icons
questions.answer: Religiously themed visual art vs. religious use/veneration of icons

questions.question: Which two authors does he name as the most famous classic defenders of icons?
options: Basil the Great and John Chrysostom
John of Damascus and Theodore the Studite
questions.answer: John of Damascus and Theodore the Studite

questions.question: What exegetical problem does he allege in John of Damascus’s claim that Moses ordered no images because of Israel’s hardness of heart?
options: It contradicts the view that God wrote the second commandment with his own finger
questions.answer: It contradicts the view that God wrote the second commandment with his own finger

questions.question: The semiotic defense of icons, as summarized, holds that:
options: Icons are only teaching aids for children
Images are spiritually neutral if not sold for profit
Only painted wood can represent Christ validly
Veneration given to the image passes to the prototype (the one depicted)
questions.answer: Veneration given to the image passes to the prototype (the one depicted)

questions.question: In the discussion of Origen and Celsus, what point does Schooping make about ancient pagans?
options: They had no concept of representation in worship
They were aware of sophisticated ‘we honor the god through the image’ reasoning
questions.answer: They were aware of sophisticated ‘we honor the god through the image’ reasoning

questions.question: The Philocalia anthology of Origen’s material was compiled by which two figures?
options: Theodore the Studite and John of Damascus
Gregory of Nazianzus and Basil the Great
questions.answer: Gregory of Nazianzus and Basil the Great

questions.question: Who are the “three hierarchs” in Eastern Orthodoxy as he lists them?
options: Basil the Great, Gregory of Nazianzus, and John Chrysostom
questions.answer: Basil the Great, Gregory of Nazianzus, and John Chrysostom

questions.question: Jay Duma’s work on the Ten Commandments is published by which press, per the lecture?
questions.answer: Presbyterian and Reformed (P&R)

questions.question: Which early synod does he cite (via Duma) as prohibiting images and painted murals in churches?
questions.answer: Synod of Elvira

questions.question: Regarding Bishop Serenus of Marseilles, what does Schooping report Pope Gregory the Great said?
options: He praised zeal against worship of images but said Serenus did not have to destroy the images
He ordered all icons destroyed immediately
questions.answer: He praised zeal against worship of images but said Serenus did not have to destroy the images

questions.question: What definition of “anathema” does he quote from the Seventh Ecumenical Council’s letter to the emperor and empress?
options: A temporary suspension from communion only
Nothing less than complete separation from God
A request for further theological debate
A monetary fine imposed by the state
questions.answer: Nothing less than complete separation from God

questions.question: Which biblical passage does he emphasize where an angel refuses an act of proskynēsis?
questions.answer: Revelation 22:8–9

questions.question: In Rev 22, what relationship does the angel claim to John to refuse veneration?
options: The angel says he is God the Father
The angel says John is already dead
The angel says worship is only forbidden on the Sabbath
The angel says he is John’s fellow servant (syndoulos) with the prophets
questions.answer: The angel says he is John’s fellow servant (syndoulos) with the prophets

questions.question: What does Schooping say the council admits about the Greek word proskyneō?
options: It can refer to worship or to veneration (a word of many significations)
It always means latria and nothing else
It is obsolete after the Incarnation
It appears only in the Old Testament
questions.answer: It can refer to worship or to veneration (a word of many significations)

questions.question: Which council defended the use and veneration of holy images in the material Schooping discusses?
options: Second Council of Nicaea / Seventh Ecumenical Council (787)
questions.answer: Second Council of Nicaea / Seventh Ecumenical Council (787)

questions.question: Canon 13 (as cited) connects excommunication to being assigned a place where:
options: There is only silence for three days
The worm dieth not and the fire is not quenched
questions.answer: The worm dieth not and the fire is not quenched

questions.question: What excursus does Schooping use to parallel his critique of “consensus of the Fathers” on icons?
options: Early premillennialism / chiliasm in pre-Nicene and Nicene-era sources
questions.answer: Early premillennialism / chiliasm in pre-Nicene and Nicene-era sources

questions.question: Which essay collection does he recommend for the premillennialism digest?
questions.answer: Essays on Premillennialism

questions.question: What term does he use for rejection of religious image use (as distinct from destroying images)?
questions.answer: Aniconism

questions.question: Which archaeological site does he mention for early Christian religious-themed images in buildings?
questions.answer: Dura-Europos

questions.question: How does Schooping characterize bowing to created matter in worship?
options: As required for salvation in the Old Testament only
As a universal human ‘language’ of worship that enslaves and reverses the order of creation
As identical to prayer without words
questions.answer: As a universal human ‘language’ of worship that enslaves and reverses the order of creation

questions.question: What does he claim about Basil’s argument regarding honor passing from image to archetype?
options: It is misapplied when used to justify venerating created icons as Orthodox iconology does
It was originally about cult icons of Christ in churches
It proves the Seventh Council was unnecessary
It was written by Theodore the Studite
questions.answer: It is misapplied when used to justify venerating created icons as Orthodox iconology does

questions.question: Origen’s critique (as cited) includes objection to philosophers who ‘pretend’ to pray to images in order to:
options: Accommodate the multitude while being insincere
questions.answer: Accommodate the multitude while being insincere

questions.question: What pastoral closing emphasis does Schooping give regarding salvation?
options: Union with God is a free gift in Christ without needing to venerate or bow to images
Only the Seventh Council can forgive sins
Icons are optional but fasting is mandatory for all
questions.answer: Union with God is a free gift in Christ without needing to venerate or bow to images
